Title: Noam Hurwitz
Description: Modern chips are produced in part through lithography: light is projected through a mask onto photosensitive material coating silicon to pattern transistors, contacts, vias, interconnects, and other structures. As Moore’s Law pushes on and chips continue to pack more computational capacity into smaller surface areas, lithography machines need increasingly fine precision.
